subject: math
instructions: Take your time and show your work. Do the warm-up problems first, then the dragon word problems. If one feels tricky, take a breath and try a smaller step — you've got this!

questions.prompt: Dragon problem: A young trainer feeds her dragon 3/4 of a barrel of fish in the morning and 2/3 of a barrel at night. How many barrels does the dragon eat in one day? (Write your answer as a mixed number.)
Dragon problem: A dragon flies 4.6 miles to a hidden cove, then takes a longer path 3.85 miles back home. How far did it fly in all?
Dragon problem: A speedy dragon can fly 12 miles in 1/4 of an hour. At that speed, how far can it fly in a full hour?

Dragon problem (two steps): The Dragon Academy has 6 baskets of fish, with 2.5 pounds in each basket. (a) How many pounds of fish is that in all? (b) If 3 dragons share it equally, how many pounds does each dragon get?
